programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go North
programme.desc: Simon King and Dave Myers explore Cumbria and the Lake District. The Lakes is the most visited national park in England, and with such incredible scenery, its not hard to see why. Dave takes Si around his beloved home turf in search of the best produce and food that this beautiful place can offer, cooking a fantastic Cumbrian breakfast, a summer vegetable strudel and a Thai feast along the way

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond celebrates the opening of her new sweet shop. For the occasions, she makes an incredible jewelled divinity and two over-the-top ice cream sundaes

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond makes a dinner to-go for a new cowboy on the ranch. The main course is a chicken jalapeno popper casserole, followed by pretzel rolls and watermelon

programme.title: Jamies Quick and Easy Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver offers viewers the ultimate set of go-to recipes for quick and easy-to-remember cooking having developed a collection of tasty dishes that each use just five ingredients, producing stress-free meals perfect for busy days and for those people who dont have the time or energy to devote to complicated cooking. On the menu in the first edition are seared scallops with black pudding, mashed potatoes and peas, as well as egg and mango chutney flatbreads, rib-eye steak, and an almond pastry puff

programme.title: Jamies Quick and Easy Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver demonstrates four more stress-free recipes that each use only five ingredients each, including sesame seared tuna, a messy meatball bun and chocolate rye cookies. To top it all off, he prepares a hearty chicken pot pie, a comforting classic with a quick and easy hack for the perfect crispy crust

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa - Back to Basics
programme.desc: Na Garten shares her vegetable based recipes for main courses, starters, salads and side-dishes

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa - Back to Basics
programme.desc: American chef and cookbook writer Ina Garten samples a dinner of chicken with wild mushrooms and green salad, before cooking a baked goats cheese at Sinskeys vineyard

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Twelve contestants battle it out to be named best amateur baker of 2014, beginning by tackling a Swiss roll, a cherry cake and miniature British classics. Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins present and judges Mary Berry and Paul Hollywood cast a critical eye over the contenders efforts

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Having shown they have what it takes to bake a cake or two, the 11 remaining contestants turn their attention to biscuits. First up they create their signature savoury crackers for cheese, and are encouraged to push the flavour. In the technical challenge, they have to prepare Mary Berrys Florentines, with their chewy caramel sauce and brandy-snap texture, before ending with a showstopper like no other - three-dimensional scenes made entirely from biscuits. For one hopeful, there will be the pride of being crowned Star Baker, while another will be sent home - well aware that thats just the way the cookie crumbles. Hosted by Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: The chefs culinary tour of France leads him to the diverse and distinct region of the Camargue, a natural region located south of Arles. James begins at Les Marquises, the Laurent familys farm, where he learns about the Camargue bull, and barbecues local fish for the family. Later, he visits a rice farm and cooks a risotto using his own rice-brewed beer

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef arrives in the city of Arles, famous for its Roman architecture and the largest market in Provence. While there James sets up his mobile kitchen to cook a dish of John Dory with tomatoes, courgettes and clams - all from the stalls around him. He also has a go at pottery with a local ceramicist, visits an award-winning wine producer - who causes him to rethink the merits of rose wine - and heads to an olive grove where he cooks fried lamb chops with baby aubergines

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go North
programme.desc: Simon King and Dave Myers explore Cumbria and the Lake District. The Lakes is the most visited national park in England, and with such incredible scenery, its not hard to see why. Dave takes Si around his beloved home turf in search of the best produce and food that this beautiful place can offer, cooking a fantastic Cumbrian breakfast, a summer vegetable strudel and a Thai feast along the way

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond celebrates the opening of her new sweet shop. For the occasions, she makes an incredible jewelled divinity and two over-the-top ice cream sundaes

programme.desc: Ree Drummond makes a dinner to-go for a new cowboy on the ranch. The main course is a chicken jalapeno popper casserole, followed by pretzel rolls and watermelon

programme.title: Jamies Quick and Easy Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver offers viewers the ultimate set of go-to recipes for quick and easy-to-remember cooking having developed a collection of tasty dishes that each use just five ingredients, producing stress-free meals perfect for busy days and for those people who dont have the time or energy to devote to complicated cooking. On the menu in the first edition are seared scallops with black pudding, mashed potatoes and peas, as well as egg and mango chutney flatbreads, rib-eye steak, and an almond pastry puff

programme.title: Jamies Quick and Easy Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver demonstrates four more stress-free recipes that each use only five ingredients each, including sesame seared tuna, a messy meatball bun and chocolate rye cookies. To top it all off, he prepares a hearty chicken pot pie, a comforting classic with a quick and easy hack for the perfect crispy crust

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Twelve contestants battle it out to be named best amateur baker of 2014, beginning by tackling a Swiss roll, a cherry cake and miniature British classics. Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins present and judges Mary Berry and Paul Hollywood cast a critical eye over the contenders efforts

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Having shown they have what it takes to bake a cake or two, the 11 remaining contestants turn their attention to biscuits. First up they create their signature savoury crackers for cheese, and are encouraged to push the flavour. In the technical challenge, they have to prepare Mary Berrys Florentines, with their chewy caramel sauce and brandy-snap texture, before ending with a showstopper like no other - three-dimensional scenes made entirely from biscuits. For one hopeful, there will be the pride of being crowned Star Baker, while another will be sent home - well aware that thats just the way the cookie crumbles. Hosted by Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins

programme.title: Jamies Quick and Easy Food

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa - Back to Basics
programme.desc: Ina Garten cooks with Grand Pellegrino for the famous Raos restaurant, and finds out the best kitchens in America

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The 10 remaining contestants face three tasks designed to test their bread-making skills, beginning by creating 12 perfect rye bread rolls shaped in any way they like and using as much flour as they dare. For the technical challenge they have to prepare Italian ciabatta using Pauls recipe, before ending with show-stopping centrepiece loaves that will have to make an impact on the table as well as on the judges. Paul Hollywood and Mary Berry then decide which of the contenders will be the weeks star baker - and which of them is going home. Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins present

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Judges Mary Berry and Paul Hollywood assess the remaining contestants dessert-making skills, beginning with a signature challenge of a sponge containing a gooey sauce filling or a saucy surprise at the bottom. The technical round involves creating a tiramisu cake, before the contenders have to prepare a baked Alaska for the showstopper. Plus, presenter Sue Perkins explores the origins of Paignton pudding, a heavyweight version of which caused a riot in the Devon town in 1859

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef arrives in the riverside town of LIsle-sur-la-Sorgue in south-eastern France, where his culinary hero Keith Floyd once owned a restaurant. Buying produce from its floating market, and a Floyd-style hat, James sets up his mobile kitchen on a bridge overlooking the river, and cooks peaches pain perdu in the sunshine. Heading back in the direction of the town, James samples some truffle-laden dishes at renowned restaurant La Beaugraviere

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef heads to Normandy and sets up his mobile kitchen in one of the many calvados orchards in the area, making a dish of artichokes with hollandaise. He also visits restaurant Le Pave dAuge in Beuvron-en-Auge to meets a chef who likes to cook with as much butter as he does

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: The chefs culinary tour of France leads him to the diverse and distinct region of the Camargue, a natural region located south of Arles. James begins at Les Marquises, the Laurent familys farm, where he learns about the Camargue bull, and barbecues local fish for the family. Later, he visits a rice farm and cooks a risotto using his own rice-brewed beer

programme.title: James Martins French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef arrives in the city of Arles, famous for its Roman architecture and the largest market in Provence. While there James sets up his mobile kitchen to cook a dish of John Dory with tomatoes, courgettes and clams - all from the stalls around him. He also has a go at pottery with a local ceramicist, visits an award-winning wine producer - who causes him to rethink the merits of rose wine - and heads to an olive grove where he cooks fried lamb chops with baby aubergines

programme.desc: Jamie Oliver demonstrates more stress-free recipes for tasty dishes that each contain only five ingredients, making quick Asian fishcakes with a brilliant tip for a sweet and spicy glaze. He also prepares a speedy lemony courgette linguine, sticky lamb chops as a single-pan dish, and cherry chocolate mousse for dessert

programme.title: Jamies Quick and Easy Food
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver demonstrates more stress-free recipes for tasty dishes that each contain only five ingredients, making a tender lamb shoulder for a sharing feast that is super-quick to prepare, as well as green spaghetti, a one-pan fish dish and apple crumble cookies.

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa - Back to Basics
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at what some chefs cook when they get home after work, with quick and easy recipes

programme.title: Barefoot Contessa - Back to Basics
programme.desc: Ina Garten cooks with Grand Pellegrino for the famous Raos restaurant, and finds out the best kitchens in America

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: The 10 remaining contestants face three tasks designed to test their bread-making skills, beginning by creating 12 perfect rye bread rolls shaped in any way they like and using as much flour as they dare. For the technical challenge they have to prepare Italian ciabatta using Pauls recipe, before ending with show-stopping centrepiece loaves that will have to make an impact on the table as well as on the judges. Paul Hollywood and Mary Berry then decide which of the contenders will be the weeks star baker - and which of them is going home. Mel Giedroyc and Sue Perkins present

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Judges Mary Berry and Paul Hollywood assess the remaining contestants dessert-making skills, beginning with a signature challenge of a sponge containing a gooey sauce filling or a saucy surprise at the bottom. The technical round involves creating a tiramisu cake, before the contenders have to prepare a baked Alaska for the showstopper. Plus, presenter Sue Perkins explores the origins of Paignton pudding, a heavyweight version of which caused a riot in the Devon town in 1859

programme.title: Paul Hollywood: City Bakes
programme.desc: Paul looks back over his incredible journey into the baking of the worlds cities, and showcases some of the most inspirational characters that he met on his travels

programme.title: Paul Hollywood: City Bakes
programme.desc: Paul looks back over his incredible journey into the baking of the worlds cities, and showcases some of his favourite moments spent with the bakers themselves

programme.title: Jamie Oliver: Cooking for Less
programme.desc: With the rising cost of food and energy, Jamie Oliver is on a mission to make delicious dishes that wont break the budget

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri visits a sibling-run Vietnamese restaurant in Holladay, Utah where they are serving up honey glazed pork vermicelli noodle buns

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Guy Fieri searches for an authentic taste of Oahu in Hawaii as he travels to a family-run Filipino restaurant serving up pork adobo fried rice

programme.title: Little House on the Prairie
programme.desc: Charles wants to seed a new field and decides to obtain a loan from the new banker, Ebenezer Sprague. The cold-hearted gentleman refuses to trust Charles and his family until Laura unwittingly befriends him at the fishing hole. Their friendship and its abrupt end shows the banker that no man should worry about making any true friends

programme.title: Little House on the Prairie
programme.desc: A talented player leaves the townsfolk convinced they will win an annual baseball game. Star pitcher Mumford appears to be an ace up Walnut Groves sleeve, until his wife discovers the townspeople are betting heavily on the games outcome. To put a stop to the madness, she benches him, leaving Caroline to persuade Mrs Mumford to allow him to play. The troubles of the Walnut Grove team are not over, however, between arguments of the rightful name of the team, to Sleepy Eyes Green Stockings win-at-any-cost attitude, the battle for the game is fought in quite an unorthodox manner

programme.title: The Waltons
programme.desc: John-Boy is sent to report on the landing of the Hindenburg, but is traumatised when he witnesses its destruction and the deaths of many passengers

programme.title: Born and Bred
programme.desc: The tension between Tom and Arthur reaches breaking point, and it seems a journey to New Zealand is on the cards for one of the doctors. programme.title: Highway to Heaven
programme.desc: Mark mistakenly thinks Jonathan has been turned into a dog, and helps to reunite a young runaway with his family

programme.title: Little House on the Prairie
programme.desc: When Nellie dares Laura to enter a rickety old house, Laura discovers mysterious hermit Amos Pike living there. She learns that he is waiting for his beloved wife, an actress, to return home. Laura excitedly volunteers to help in fixing up the place until a trip to Mankato shows that his wife is in fact dead and not coming back. Laura then must help him overcome his denial and sorrow, when she tells him that his wife is dead

programme.title: Little House on the Prairie
programme.desc: Laura wants to go to the annual Spring Dance with Henry Henderson, while Grace Snider hopes Mr Edwards will ask her out. However, both men are too shy to ask the girls. Caroline decides to teach them a technique she used on Charles to make him jealous. While the two girls shamelessly deceive their beaus, Charles reminds Caroline about the exact circumstances that occurred when she tried it on him

programme.title: Little House on the Prairie
programme.desc: Part one of two. A widow realises she does not have long to live, so asks Charles to find homes for her three children

programme.title: The Waltons
programme.desc: Grandma is admitted to hospital, but Grandpa does not cope well with being away from her. Corabeth thinks Elizabeth is a bad influence on Aimee, and forbids them from being friends

programme.title: The Waltons
programme.desc: The Baldwin sisters niece returns to America after living in Germany, and John Boy suspects she is running away from something terrible that happened there

programme.title: Ballykissangel
programme.desc: Strange goings-on in the atmosphere attract a ufologist to the village, but the brewing storm has disastrous consequences for Sean, sending a tree crashing through the roof of his house which narrowly misses his daughter. James Ellis guest stars

programme.title: Born and Bred
programme.sub-title: And Is There Honey Still for Tea?
programme.desc: Richard Wilson joins the cast as a new doctor who arrives in Ormston, much to the dismay of Tom who learns the newcomers timely appearance has been engineered by his father. Their working relationship gets off to a bad start too, when they bicker over how to treat a case of polio. Elsewhere, Wilfs plans for a double wedding are nipped in the bud by Jean, and the Cosgroves struggle to cope with their foster-daughters hi-jinks, while Deborah and Phyllis unearth the truth behind the village-wide eviction notices

programme.desc: Lady Coras pregnant maid is rushed in with labour pains, causing Roberts reluctant return

programme.title: Highway to Heaven
programme.desc: A young man learns that the brother he once considered institutionalising has a brilliant mind within a paralysed body. Michael Landon stars

programme.title: Highway to Heaven
programme.desc: Mark mistakenly thinks Jonathan has been turned into a dog, and helps to reunite a young runaway with his family

programme.title: Little House on the Prairie
programme.desc: When Nellie dares Laura to enter a rickety old house, Laura discovers mysterious hermit Amos Pike living there. She learns that he is waiting for his beloved wife, an actress, to return home. Laura excitedly volunteers to help in fixing up the place until a trip to Mankato shows that his wife is in fact dead and not coming back. Laura then must help him overcome his denial and sorrow, when she tells him that his wife is dead

programme.title: Little House on the Prairie
programme.desc: Laura wants to go to the annual Spring Dance with Henry Henderson, while Grace Snider hopes Mr Edwards will ask her out. However, both men are too shy to ask the girls. Caroline decides to teach them a technique she used on Charles to make him jealous. While the two girls shamelessly deceive their beaus, Charles reminds Caroline about the exact circumstances that occurred when she tried it on him

programme.title: Little House on the Prairie
programme.desc: Part one of two. A widow realises she does not have long to live, so asks Charles to find homes for her three children

programme.title: The Waltons
programme.desc: Grandma is admitted to hospital, but Grandpa does not cope well with being away from her. Corabeth thinks Elizabeth is a bad influence on Aimee, and forbids them from being friends

programme.title: The Waltons
programme.desc: The Baldwin sisters niece returns to America after living in Germany, and John Boy suspects she is running away from something terrible that happened there

programme.title: Ballykissangel
programme.desc: Strange goings-on in the atmosphere attract a ufologist to the village, but the brewing storm has disastrous consequences for Sean, sending a tree crashing through the roof of his house which narrowly misses his daughter. James Ellis guest stars

programme.title: Born and Bred
programme.sub-title: And Is There Honey Still for Tea?
programme.desc: Richard Wilson joins the cast as a new doctor who arrives in Ormston, much to the dismay of Tom who learns the newcomers timely appearance has been engineered by his father. Their working relationship gets off to a bad start too, when they bicker over how to treat a case of polio. Elsewhere, Wilfs plans for a double wedding are nipped in the bud by Jean, and the Cosgroves struggle to cope with their foster-daughters hi-jinks, while Deborah and Phyllis unearth the truth behind the village-wide eviction notices

programme.title: Bramwell
programme.desc: Lady Coras pregnant maid is rushed in with labour pains, causing Roberts reluctant return

programme.title: Rumpole of the Bailey
programme.desc: Racial purist Rex Parkin is accused of inciting a riot and Rumpole must cut through the tense courtroom atmosphere to find the truth. However, additional friction is added to the mix when the accused realises that Rumpole has taken a pupil barrister from the Punjab under his wing. Drama, with Leo McKern

programme.title: M*A*S*H
programme.desc: The men are asked by TV interviewer Clete Roberts about their feelings on the war and its effects on them, and their futures for a special documentary. Korean War comedy, starring Alan Alda and Larry Linville

programme.desc: Part one of two. Rumours of a Chinese offensive force the 4077th to retreat, leaving Hawkeye, BJ and Hot Lips to complete a difficult operation. Korean War comedy, starring Alan Alda, Gary Burghoff and Loretta Swit

programme.desc: Part two of two. Chinese forces close in, leaving Hawkeye, BJ and Hot Lips with a badly injured patient. Korean War comedy, starring Alan Alda, Gary Burgh and Loretta Swit

Western, directed by and starring Kirk Douglas. With Bruce Dern and Bo Hopkins

However, he exploits the opportunity to set up a profitable sideline trafficking drugs for a cartel. His dangerous double life takes a darker turn when he is ordered to supply weapons to far-right rebel groups operating in Nicaragua. Fact-based comedy drama, starring Tom Cruise and Domhnall Gleeson

programme.desc: A bank manager teams up with his ex-cop neighbour to hunt down the thieves who killed a friend in a botched heist. Action thriller, starring Frank Grillo, Bruce Willis and Johnathon Schaech

Action thriller, starring Olga Kurylenko, Morgan Freeman and James Purefoy

programme.desc: Unconventional submarine skipper Joe Glass is sent to investigate the torpedoing of US and Russian vessels. He winds up trying to rescue the Russian president, who has been kidnapped by a rogue general. Action thriller, starring Gerard Butler, Gary Oldman and Common

programme.title: Crank 2: High Voltage (2009)
programme.desc: Hitman Chev Chelios sets off in pursuit of a Chinese mobster who has removed his heart and replaced it with a machine requiring regular jolts of electricity to keep it working. As he tries to locate his missing organ, he must resort to increasingly elaborate methods of obtaining the necessary voltage to stay alive. Action thriller sequel, starring Jason Statham, Amy Smart, Dwight Yoakam and Efren Ramirez

programme.title: Never Let Go (2015)
programme.desc: A woman on holiday in Morocco takes the law into her own hands after her baby is kidnapped, but ends up framed for murder. Thriller, with Angela Dixon and Nigel Whitmey

programme.title: The Internecine Project (1974)
programme.desc: An economics professor is about to start a prestigious job as a government adviser - but his murky past as an industrial spy threatens his chances. His only way of burying the past is to dispose of his former associates, so he hatches a plan to trick them into murdering one another. Thriller, starring James Coburn, Lee Grant, Harry Andrews and Ian Hendry

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Sarah, 35, arrives at Kings College Hospital concerned that a brain tumour she first had as a teenager has returned. Helen, 47, is worried about her baby son James, who was born with a hole in his heart and one kidney, and has been vomiting since the morning, while 28-year-old Wayne seeks treatment for knife wounds to his leg after he tried to stop someone stealing his girlfriends phone

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics are called out to a young woman whos had a panic attack while driving to work and pulls over in the middle of rush-hour traffic. An elderly woman who feared her sons van was being broken into rushes outside and slips on the driveway. A man in a community centre who speaks very little English is having chest pains. Hes on medication for a previous heart attack but has not renewed his prescription

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: The staff of Kings College Hospital reflect on the different ways in which people deal with physical and emotional pain as they treat a new batch of patients. Ahmed, 28, dislocated his shoulder during a game of American football and grits his teeth as a doctor slots it back into place. Meanwhile, 24-year-old Lee is living with Behcets disease, a condition that affects his nervous system and causes pain so severe he passes out, and father-of-four Simon, 30, has been found unconscious at the bottom of a stairwell and it emerges he hasnt eaten for days following a relationship break-up

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Sarah, 35, arrives at Kings College Hospital concerned that a brain tumour she first had as a teenager has returned. Helen, 47, is worried about her baby son James, who was born with a hole in his heart and one kidney, and has been vomiting since the morning, while 28-year-old Wayne seeks treatment for knife wounds to his leg after he tried to stop someone stealing his girlfriends phone

programme.title: Strangers on a Plane
programme.desc: For the last day in Porto, horse-loving Louise from Walkington takes her rivals riding - but not everyone is keen to saddle up. In the evening, she organises a walking tour of the city, after which the favourite is revealed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants head to Lynton in north Devon, where they stay at Alistair and Emma Mundells four-star Castle Hill Guesthouse. The pair only opened the BandB three months prior to the competition, and hope to impress with their recently renovated rooms, but Brigitte Tee proves hard to win over. Two of their guests are also forced to overcome their fears during a trip on a Victorian cliff railway

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: On the Isle of Wight, Henry Cole finds an unusual three-wheeled motorbike and Simon OBrien unearths an old projector screen that he wants to turn into a work of art

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lorraine and Jon want to fulfil their long-held dream of owning a house among the white-washed mountain villages of Axarquía in Spain. With a budget of £110,000, Jean Johansson is able to show them five attractive options, but will they love any of them enough to put in an offer?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In the West Midlands, the first host, company director Sue, promises an evening her guests will never forget. Joining her are business guru Marc, teaching assistant Kim, manager Paul and mole catcher Michael. Sue hopes to get things off to a great start, but when her starter reminds Kim of baby food and Marc of toilets, things begin to look bleak

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In the West Midlands, business guru Marc intends to use his coaching skills to plan an impressive night, but after annoying his fellow diners, he has a tough job ahead

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Teaching assistant Kim hopes to win over her guests with a summer holiday-themed evening, as she hosts the third dinner party of the week in the West Midlands

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: College manager and part-time comedian Paul is the fourth to play host in the West Midlands, turning his home into a pub in the hope of pulling in the points by pulling a few pints

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the last night in the West Midlands, mole-catcher Michael serves up fancy French cuisine. However, he faces an uphill battle after he chooses a controversial main course, and one of his guests vomits before theyve even started eating. Its an eventful evening, with bizarre butlers, self-penned songs and animal antics

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at Brook Meadow, near Market Harborough in Leicestershire. Host Claire is banking on the appeal of her interior design skills and picturesque location

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to the Apollo Inn in Barlborough, where host James claims his poached eggs are the best in the business - and there are certainly no complaints at breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandBers head to The Swan Inn in Letton, Herefordshire, run by Luke and best friend Heather. Their freshly renovated coaching inn has expectations high, and the guests are impressed by how sparklingly new everything is. Disappointment is the order of the day at breakfast, though, when the guests are left out in the cold by the continental offering

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Steph and Martin welcome the BandBers to White Mark Glamping in Watlington, Oxfordshire. Some of their guests arent quick to embrace the great outdoors, especially upon discovering the communal shower blocks and lack of electrics

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Its payment day, and time for the BandBers to settle some scores and find out how much theyve been paid. One BandBer comes under fire for the harsh criticisms they left throughout the week, while toilet troubles and cleanliness concerns are at the top of one pairs agenda. After opinions are aired and differences debated, the payments are revealed and the winner is finally announced

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend are off to Manchester to pick up a 1978 Bedford HA ice-cream van. This rusty wreck belongs to Barbara who has served ice-cream to the locals for more than 50 years

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend head to Leicester to pick up a Mk 3 Ford Cortina, once Britains top-selling car but now on the automotive endangered species list. The car belongs to 53-year-old Bobby whose family hope the restored car will give him the pick-me-up he needs

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp meets Stuart and Lara, who want to be under the same roof after two years of being together, but living 100 miles apart. The pair look in the vicinity of Amersham, while Phil Spencer meets Randhir and Sheetal. They want to swap London living for village life in Berkshire before baby number two arrives in five weeks

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer meet Amber and Martin Brammah in Waterbeach, Cambridgeshire, who are undecided on whether they should sell their home or completely renovate it. Amber owns a fitness company and runs classes from home, and feels a bigger property would be better for her business. However, Martin feels that it would be better to use their budget to renovate their current family home

programme.title: Great Estates from Above
programme.desc: Using cutting-edge drone and aerial photography to reveal Britains country houses and palaces from a new perspective, capturing breathtaking architecture, formal gardens and sweeping parklands in extraordinary detail. Beginning with Blenheim Palace in Oxfordshire

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Tree surgeon Kevin, 57, arrives at Kings College Hospital in London with breathing difficulties, having fallen off a ladder onto a metal bar, while 11-year-old Archie has cut his lip in an accident at school and is worried about stitches. Elsewhere, a drink-related fall has left 53-year-old John with a head injury, and barman Ross, 31, needs treatment for a fractured eye socket following an alcohol-fuelled night out

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Fifty-year-old mechanical engineer Graham is rushed into Kings College Hospital in south London with a suspected heart attack, and after his brother reminisces about their carefree youth, the doctor suggests Grahams problems may be related to his more recent stressful lifestyle. Meanwhile, 71-year-old Pat is found unconscious by her husband, and 65-year-old Mick is discovered lying in the street with blood coming out of his ear. In both cases, the medics suspect brain haemorrhages, so scans are required

programme.title: Strangers on a Plane
programme.desc: Actor Damon from Swindon takes his guests on a Segway tour of Barcelona, visiting the citys architectural hotspots. But his day is in danger of being a washout

programme.title: Four in a Bed

The pair rig Blackpool-hater Alistair Mundells room with practical jokes, and take their guests to Funny Girls drag cabaret bar, where Mark Crib confesses he has never had such a surreal time

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: In Cockermouth, Cumbria, Simon OBrien and Henry Cole meet blacksmith Jon, who spent many years working on fairgrounds. At the valuation, independent valuer Ronnie Archer-Morgan reckons theres rich pickings to be made from railway memorabilia

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lorna and Gordon are relocating and looking for their new dream home around the seaside town of Benalmádena on Spains Costa del Sol. For their budget of £250,000, they want enough space for their family of 19 children and grandchildren to come and visit. Ben Hillman shows them five great options, but will any measure up to the couples idea of perfection?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The competition comes from in and around Hastings and Rye in East Sussex, where the first host is 52-year-old nursery owner Louise, who plans a fun-filled evening of Thai food. Her guests are horse-mad Emily, whos 40, 56-year-old Caribbean takeaway owner Clive, 23-year-old beauty therapist Chiara and mature student and self-confessed foodie Gary. A power cut threatens to scupper Louises night before its even started, until her husband - and farmer - Roddy and his generator come to the rescue. Will the night impress the guests, and particularly the self-appointed Thai food expert at the table?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The second night from Hastings and Rye in East Sussex sees host Emily plan an evening of comfort food, including carrot and parsnip soup, meat loaf and apple and blackberry crumble

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Australian mature student Gary hosts the third dinner party from Hastings and Rye, East Sussex, planning an evening filled with fun, flash and fabulous flavour

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Dec, a former member of 1960s band the Bachelors, hosts the next dinner party from Hastings and Rye, planning to impress his guests with musical surprises and a simple seaside menu

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the final night in Hastings and Rye, East Sussex, beauty therapist Chiara returns to her roots to throw an Italian dinner party. However, Irish crooner Dec gets on his hosts wrong side when he questions her interest in healing crystals and likens her to a page three glamour girl

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The first visit of the week is to The Lazy Pug in Warwickshire. Friends Ash and Rosie are hoping to win over their guests with quirky rooms and a healthy dose of neon lights

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The rivals head to the Crofts Hotel in Cardiff, run by chef Lorraine. However, the guests are uninspired by the bland decoration and feel that everything is a bit basic

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The rivals spend the night at the Hollybush Inn in Priors Marston, Warwickshire, where owner Anthony hopes to wow his guests with the 16th-century coaching inns history

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The guest house owners arrive at The Pretty Thing glampsite in St Osyth, Essex, run by Phil. Theres a melee of mixed emotions when the guests arrive, with Lorraine and Anthony left feeling cold by the communal showers, while Ash and Rosie embrace all the impressive luxury their Aztec tent has to offer. Over drinks, Phil lets slip that the breakfast service the following morning will be his first ever

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Its the final day of the competition, and the BandBers reconvene to feud about facilities, bicker over beds and cross swords over sausages, before the payments are revealed and the winner announced. Garish neon lights, dogs at breakfast and cleanliness issues are on the agenda as the hoteliers get heated while discussing a colourful week

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Fuzz Townshend and Tim Shaw enter the weird and wonderful world of the Bond Bug - a wedge-shaped three-wheel car from the early 1970s. The car belongs to 69-year-old Ian, whose dreams of joining the Bond Bug owners club and taking part in rallies and car shows were cruelly thwarted when he developed Parkinsons disease. Tim and Fuzz step in to get his quirky motor up and running while he is still able to enjoy it

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend head to Wellingborough, Northamptonshire, to pick up a 1988 Fiat Uno Turbo, one of the fastest hatchbacks of its time. The car belongs to car salesman Gerry, a dyed-in-the-wool boy racer. Sadly, Gerrys ill health has meant that the Uno has been tucked away and left to rot on his drive. His friends and family hope the boys treatment can bring some joy back into Gerrys life

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp helps a couple looking for a home in Warrington, who do not seem to be able to agree on the best approach to house hunting, which has led to six months with no progress. In Liverpool, Phil Spencer comes to the aid of a successful entrepreneur looking for a house in Crosby, but who has very specific ideas

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer travel to the village of Clifford, West Yorkshire, to meet Vic and Paul Miller, who are unsure whether to sell or upgrade their house. Vic feels that the pair should look for a new home more suitable for them and their two young twins, but Paul believes they could use the budget to transform the propertys layout and create a more functional family space

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Holistic therapist Kara and electrician Jonny are building a striking stone and zinc four-bedroom family home in the rolling hills of West Yorkshire, for themselves and their two young children. The need to build has been motivated by Karas recent paralysis, caused by a rare autoimmune condition. Their ambition for the house is enormous, as Kara wants to create a home that is not only beautiful but will help her regain some independence. With money tight, Jonny needs to take on a lot of the work himself. As the build progresses, and despite the challenges coming thick and fast, compromise is not an option with both form and function so essential. Building their single storey pushes both Kara and Jonny to the edge of their emotional and physical reserves, but if they can pull it off, it might just redefine what accessible design means

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Doctors run tests on 89-year-old Alyce, who has been brought to Kings by ambulance after falling near her home. Shes accompanied by her close friend Linda, and reveals her resolve to make sure she lives long enough to celebrate her next birthday. Soon after, 23-year-old groom-to-be Sean is examined following an epileptic seizure lasting most of the night

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Among those arriving at Kings College Hospital is 56-year-old Malcolm, who is put on life support after a cardiac arrest. Nightclub host Phil, 36, receives treatment for chronic pain in his back and side, and realises his party lifestyle is taking its toll, while medics discover 61-year-old Bob has suffered a stroke. Doctors also see to Kamilla, 12, who has been knocked down by a car on a pedestrian crossing

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: On the night shift, paramedics are called to a man complaining of a fast heart rate and chest pains. In the back of the ambulance, he claims to have taken amphetamines for several days. When a man is found slumped over a table outside a shop, the owners ring 999. The patient is intoxicated, and the all-female crew must be wary of his tight grip as they treat him in the back of the ambulance

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Doctors run tests on 89-year-old Alyce, who has been brought to Kings by ambulance after falling near her home. Shes accompanied by her close friend Linda, and reveals her resolve to make sure she lives long enough to celebrate her next birthday. programme.title: Strangers on a Plane
programme.desc: Tara takes the gang clubbing in one of Barcelonas top nightclubs. Her 24 hours in charge pushes some of the group out of their comfort zone, but will her idea of holiday heaven be someones holiday hell?

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics are called out to a young woman whos had a panic attack while driving to work and pulls over in the middle of rush-hour traffic. An elderly woman who feared her sons van was being broken into rushes outside and slips on the driveway. A man in a community centre who speaks very little English is having chest pains. Hes on medication for a previous heart attack but has not renewed his prescription

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: The staff of Kings College Hospital reflect on the different ways in which people deal with physical and emotional pain as they treat a new batch of patients. Ahmed, 28, dislocated his shoulder during a game of American football and grits his teeth as a doctor slots it back into place. Meanwhile, 24-year-old Lee is living with Behcets disease, a condition that affects his nervous system and causes pain so severe he passes out, and father-of-four Simon, 30, has been found unconscious at the bottom of a stairwell and it emerges he hasnt eaten for days following a relationship break-up

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Sarah, 35, arrives at Kings College Hospital concerned that a brain tumour she first had as a teenager has returned. Helen, 47, is worried about her baby son James, who was born with a hole in his heart and one kidney, and has been vomiting since the morning, while 28-year-old Wayne seeks treatment for knife wounds to his leg after he tried to stop someone stealing his girlfriends phone

programme.title: Strangers on a Plane
programme.desc: For the last day in Porto, horse-loving Louise from Walkington takes her rivals riding - but not everyone is keen to saddle up. In the evening, she organises a walking tour of the city, after which the favourite is revealed

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants head to Lynton in north Devon, where they stay at Alistair and Emma Mundells four-star Castle Hill Guesthouse. The pair only opened the BandB three months prior to the competition, and hope to impress with their recently renovated rooms, but Brigitte Tee proves hard to win over. Two of their guests are also forced to overcome their fears during a trip on a Victorian cliff railway

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: On the Isle of Wight, Henry Cole finds an unusual three-wheeled motorbike and Simon OBrien unearths an old projector screen that he wants to turn into a work of art

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lorraine and Jon want to fulfil their long-held dream of owning a house among the white-washed mountain villages of Axarquía in Spain. With a budget of £110,000, Jean Johansson is able to show them five attractive options, but will they love any of them enough to put in an offer?

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In the West Midlands, the first host, company director Sue, promises an evening her guests will never forget. Joining her are business guru Marc, teaching assistant Kim, manager Paul and mole catcher Michael. Sue hopes to get things off to a great start, but when her starter reminds Kim of baby food and Marc of toilets, things begin to look bleak

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In the West Midlands, business guru Marc intends to use his coaching skills to plan an impressive night, but after annoying his fellow diners, he has a tough job ahead

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Teaching assistant Kim hopes to win over her guests with a summer holiday-themed evening, as she hosts the third dinner party of the week in the West Midlands

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: College manager and part-time comedian Paul is the fourth to play host in the West Midlands, turning his home into a pub in the hope of pulling in the points by pulling a few pints

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On the last night in the West Midlands, mole-catcher Michael serves up fancy French cuisine. However, he faces an uphill battle after he chooses a controversial main course, and one of his guests vomits before theyve even started eating. Its an eventful evening, with bizarre butlers, self-penned songs and animal antics

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at Brook Meadow, near Market Harborough in Leicestershire. Host Claire is banking on the appeal of her interior design skills and picturesque location

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to the Apollo Inn in Barlborough, where host James claims his poached eggs are the best in the business - and there are certainly no complaints at breakfast

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandBers head to The Swan Inn in Letton, Herefordshire, run by Luke and best friend Heather. Their freshly renovated coaching inn has expectations high, and the guests are impressed by how sparklingly new everything is. Disappointment is the order of the day at breakfast, though, when the guests are left out in the cold by the continental offering

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Steph and Martin welcome the BandBers to White Mark Glamping in Watlington, Oxfordshire. Some of their guests arent quick to embrace the great outdoors, especially upon discovering the communal shower blocks and lack of electrics

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Its payment day, and time for the BandBers to settle some scores and find out how much theyve been paid. One BandBer comes under fire for the harsh criticisms they left throughout the week, while toilet troubles and cleanliness concerns are at the top of one pairs agenda. After opinions are aired and differences debated, the payments are revealed and the winner is finally announced

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend are off to Manchester to pick up a 1978 Bedford HA ice-cream van. This rusty wreck belongs to Barbara who has served ice-cream to the locals for more than 50 years

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend head to Leicester to pick up a Mk 3 Ford Cortina, once Britains top-selling car but now on the automotive endangered species list. The car belongs to 53-year-old Bobby whose family hope the restored car will give him the pick-me-up he needs

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp meets Stuart and Lara, who want to be under the same roof after two years of being together, but living 100 miles apart. The pair look in the vicinity of Amersham, while Phil Spencer meets Randhir and Sheetal. They want to swap London living for village life in Berkshire before baby number two arrives in five weeks

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer meet Amber and Martin Brammah in Waterbeach, Cambridgeshire, who are undecided on whether they should sell their home or completely renovate it. Amber owns a fitness company and runs classes from home, and feels a bigger property would be better for her business. However, Martin feels that it would be better to use their budget to renovate their current family home

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actor Nigel Havers delves into his family history, and soon discovers his ancestors are very different to the upper-class characters for which he is known. He begins by following his mothers line, an investigation that takes him to rural Cornwall, where he uncovers a story of illegitimacy worthy of a period drama. Moving to his fathers side, Nigel finds himself in Colchester for the tale of the Victorian equivalent of an Essex cab driver made good, but which has an unhappy ending

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell revisit the stories of three people navigating new relationships and complicated feelings, including ex-footballer Dominic Matteo, who was desperate to find his birth mother after being diagnosed with a brain tumour. Grant Williams returns to Derry to spend more time with his daughter Wendy OHagan to make the most of their time together in light of his recent diagnosis of Alzheimers, while Colin Deering - who first met his brother Bryan back in 2014 - starts out on a new journey to find out about his birth father

The road trip pit crew leaps into action when smoke appears inside the classic car, and all fingers point to Charles for this minor mishap. Plus, Christina detours to visit Northampton, the shoemaking capital of Britain

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Medical doctors, science broadcasters and identical twins Chris and Xand van Tulleken team up to research their family tree. They grew up surrounded by old paintings and documents commemorating characters from their Dutch ancestry, but despite this wealth of family archive, the twins know little about their heritage. Their journey takes them back to the Netherlands, where they look into the career of sea captain Jan Tulleken, their four-times great-grandfather, a staunch royalist who joined the Dutch navy at 13. But as they trace the story back, they reveal a troubling story

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actor Nigel Havers delves into his family history, and soon discovers his ancestors are very different to the upper-class characters for which he is known. He begins by following his mothers line, an investigation that takes him to rural Cornwall, where he uncovers a story of illegitimacy worthy of a period drama. Moving to his fathers side, Nigel finds himself in Colchester for the tale of the Victorian equivalent of an Essex cab driver made good, but which has an unhappy ending

programme.desc: Jack celebrates rural England, blending folklore, crafts, and gentle storytelling as he explores vanishing traditions, landscapes, and the quiet wisdom of country life.

programme.desc: Novice kayakers Tessa Fleming and Irial Ó Ceallaigh nervously prepare to take on one of Irelands most daunting and precarious sea crossings. They find out about the Gaelic culture of the island and following a disrupted sleep and another treacherous Atlantic crossing they are relieved to arrive back on the mainland. In Irish

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Honey meets Bea in the cafe and asks her to move out of No.18, but Bea heads to McClunkys and orders everyone to leave before trashing the shop when her emotions get the better of her. Zoe is blindsided, while the Panesars and the Brannings attempt to bond

programme.desc: As crops near harvest, customer numbers fall and finances tighten, and a fundamental tension emerges between growing food and selling it. programme.title: Wolf Hall
programme.desc: Return of the historical drama, adapting the final novel in Hilary Mantels trilogy about Henry VIIIs adviser Thomas Cromwell. As Anne Boleyns blood is sluiced from the scaffold, Cromwell picks his way through the wreckage of her death to take his place as principal councillor to King Henry VIII. But in rising so high, Cromwell has also attracted powerful enemies. Mark Rylance and Damian Lewis reprise their roles alongside Harriet Walter and Timonthy Spall

programme.title: Agatha Christies Murder Is Easy
programme.desc: As Luke Fitzwilliam uncovers bitter tensions between the Wychwood locals, hes convinced he has his suspect. But when a lynchpin of the community dies, his theory falters. programme.title: RTÉ News: Six One and Weather
programme.desc: The latest national and international headlines

programme.title: Nationwide
programme.desc: Presenter Colm Flynn meets a Cork man who has been promoting and supporting Irish Country singers at home and around the world

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Penny tells Vinny he doesnt need to buy her expensive gifts in return for her love, but she is shocked when Vinny reveals he has put a deposit down on a shop in Turpin Road for her. The Slaters put on a show of family unity, and Bea lies that she was attacked to garner sympathy

programme.title: Eva Paus Asian Kitchen
programme.desc: Eva Pau cooks a Chinese baked pork chop rice, a nostalgic, hearty dish layered with pork, fluffy rice, and a rich sauce. Next, she brings a twist to a classic with beef massaman curry pie, combining deep, warming curry flavours with flaky pastry. The chef also prepares Chinese chicken noodle soup

programme.title: Nevens Ulster Food Tour
programme.desc: Neven Maguire visits Glenarm Castle in Ballymena where he wanders around the gardens with estate manager Adrian Morrow, and also sees their famous shorthorn beef cows. He also takes a trip to Morellis ice cream shop in Portrush, where he samples some of their award winning flavours. Nevens own recipe is vanilla and hazelnut no-churn ice cream with poached pears and chocolate sauce

programme.title: RTÉ News: Nine OClock and Weather
programme.desc: The latest national and international news

programme.title: Blue Lights
programme.desc: Drama following three new recruits to Belfasts police force. The arrest of young drug dealer Sandy McKnight begins to expose a criminal conspiracy involving every level of Belfasts society, from wealthy businesswoman Dana Morgan to vulnerable teenager Lindsay Singleton.

programme.title: The Great House Revival
programme.desc: Architect Hugh Wallace helps Roscommon couple Mary Morris and Colm Dowd are creating their first home together - the Old Bank in Elphin. The residence is a neglected space that has allowed damp and mould to thrive. However, Mary and Colm have a vision that lets them see past all the moisture, allowing them to turn this behemoth of a building into not only into their home, but also their sanctuary

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Cyril faces his toughest challenge yet when he must make the difficult decision of whether two children abandoned in the local church will be taken into foster care or returned to their heavily pregnant mother. Matthews financial woes come to a head when he is summoned to a board meeting with his mother about the state of the business. As the summer draws to a close, the pupil midwives are coming to the end of their training and prepare to sit their final exams

programme.title: DJ Carey: The Dodger
programme.desc: The concluding part of the documentary reveals how, at the pinnacle of his fame, Careys marriage came to an end and the media pursued him relentlessly. When he met business magnate Sarah Newman, a new life in the exclusive K Club beckoned - but soon, questions about his health and finances became unavoidable, forcing him to face the music

programme.title: Camogie: Inside the Championship
programme.desc: Part two of two. Following the All Ireland Camogie Championship from start to finish, combining multiple teams and stories, witnessing heartbreak and triumph, culminating in a winner-takes-all climax at Croker

programme.title: Unwrapped: The Toy Show Appeal
programme.desc: A celebration of the joy, magic and real-life impact of The Late Late Toy Show Appeal on children and families across Ireland

programme.title: High Road, Low Road
programme.desc: Dancing with the Stars judge Karen Byrne and presenter James Patrice are in Klaipeda, Lithuania, for dunes, canoes, and a laugh or two. One of them enjoys a first-class luxury experience, while the other braves a budget trip

programme.title: Cheap Irish Homes
programme.desc: Maggie Molloy and building engineer Kieran McCarthy help 28-year-old health care worker Claire Hassett search for an affordable home. With a maximum budget of 170,000 euros, she is looking for a two or three-bedroom house that doesnt need a major refurbishment. Claire is hoping for a home that close to her job in Cork and family in Thurles

programme.title: At Your Service
programme.desc: Francis and John Brennan help Alan and Grace Skehan, who own a busy holiday resort Dunmore East, Co Waterford, with a large mobile home park, self-catering houses, a nine-hole golf course and a restaurant and bar. Despite their workload Alan and Grace think big and want the Brennans advice about their ambitious plan to build luxury lodges and to extend the resort further

programme.desc: Part one of two. programme.desc: A look at the life and career of the English actor, who won an Academy Award for his role in Gandhi and also starred in Schindlers List, Sexy Beast and Iron Man 3

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.desc: When an acid-tongued gossip columnist lets fly with another barrage of witty one-liners, his victims want revenge. Luckily, a pair of young actors are on hand to teach him a lesson

programme.title: Tales of the Unexpected
programme.sub-title: A Girl Cant Always Have Everything
programme.desc: A selfish actress used to having her own way asks her friend for help - but may be expecting too much after catching the eye of a rich, elderly widower. Joan Collins and Pauline Collins star

programme.desc: Cissie Enright is not exactly thrilled with the idea of poisoning an estate agent, but her sister Joanna insists that it is the only way

programme.title: Beyond the Brush
programme.desc: Siân Walters and James Payne explore the story behind Francisco Goyas Saturn Devouring His Son, revealing a dark imagination, turbulent times and haunting visions of human nature

programme.title: Beyond the Brush

programme.title: Arts Wildest Movement: Mannerism
programme.desc: Waldemar Januszczak looks at the latter stages of mannerism when the Sack of Rome in 1527 forced many Italian artists to flee abroad, taking the revolutionary art movement with them

programme.title: Boy George and Culture Club
programme.desc: A profile of the band, with members Boy George, Roy Hay, Mikey Craig and Jon Moss talking about the personal relationships behind the scenes

programme.title: Classic Albums

programme.title: The Strange Case of Dr Jekyll and Mr Hyde
programme.desc: Concerned by Dr Jekylls behaviour, Gabriel Utterson works to uncover the identity of the dangerous Mr Hyde. National Theatre of Scotlands retelling of RL Stevensons gothic tale

programme.title: House of the Dragon

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming examines the hidden histories of cities, revealing the sights and sounds rarely witnessed by the public. He also gets a guided tour of Soho from broadcaster Danny Baker

programme.desc: While Daenerys meets her future, Bran meets the past. Elsewhere, Tommen confronts the High Sparrow, and Arya continues her training. Starring Emilia Clarke, Maisie Williams and Isaac Hempstead-Wright

programme.title: In Treatment
programme.desc: Paul arrives at Ginas office, nervously awaiting the verdict of the court hearing into his part in Alexs suicide. As they discuss the traumatic events he has been through over the past year, he reveals he wants to leave therapy - and though he tries to make excuses, Gina believes she knows the real reason for his decision. Drama, starring Gabriel Byrne and Dianne Wiest

programme.title: The Guest Wing
programme.desc: Lord and Lady Fitzalan Howard, the owners of Carlton Towers near Selby in North Yorkshire, prepare to host a fitness boot camp weekend at their property - while also welcoming an array of guests to celebrate their sons birthday. Meanwhile, Sir Benjamin Slade hopes a murder mystery weekend at Maunsel House in Somerset will help pay off a sizeable electricity bill

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming explores the history of Bristol, beginning by learning a grisly secret at the docks before meeting Mercury Prize-winning musician Roni Size to discuss life in the St Pauls and Stokes Croft areas of the city. He also explores the district of Clifton and visits a speakeasy-style bar

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming visits Liverpool, cutting through cliches about football and the Beatles to explore its less-celebrated treasures. Among his destinations are a 1930s banking building that has remained unaltered since closing in 2007, a well-appointed set of toilets and a recording studio hidden in a bar. He also discovers which former dictator once spent Christmas in the city

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Arya returns to Winterfell and is reunited with Sansa and Bran, while Daenerys takes matters into her own hands and launches a surprise attack on the Lannister army. Jaime and Bronn collect the spoils from the war with the Tyrells

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Daenerys demands loyalty from the surviving Lannister soldiers and makes an offer to the lords of Westeros, while Jon heeds Brans warning about white walkers on the move. Cersei vows to vanquish anyone or anything that stands in her way

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: At Winterfell, Littlefinger schemes to isolate Sansa, while tensions between Arya and her ssister increase after the discovery of a letter. Jon and his men go beyond the wall to capture a white walker, while Daenerys has to make a tough decision

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Tyrion tries to save Westeros from itself as everyone meets in Kings Landing to discuss the fate of the realm, Sansa confronts Arya, while Sam reaches Winterfell, where he and Bran discover a shocking secret about Jon

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: The Wall, which has protected the Seven Kingdoms for 8,000 years, has fallen, and the Night Kings army of the dead marches towards Westeros. Meanwhile, Jon and Daenerys arrive in Winterfell, and the Stark children are reunited. Starring Emilia Clarke, Kit Harington and Peter Dinklage

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.sub-title: A Knight of the Seven Kingdoms
programme.desc: After arriving at Winterfell, Jaime Lannister is confronted with the consequences of his past mistakes. Starring Emilia Clarke, Kit Harington and Peter Dinklage

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: The Night King and the army of the dead reach Winterfell, and an epic battle between the living and the dead begins. Starring Emilia Clarke, Kit Harington and Peter Dinklage

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: After the Battle of Winterfell, the Great War to gain control over the mythical land of Westeros continues as Jon and Daenerys look to the south. Meanwhile, Jon shares a secret with his siblings. Starring Emilia Clarke, Kit Harington and Peter Dinklage

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Varys betrays his queen, and Daenerys brings her forces to Kings Landing as the explosive final battle for the Iron Throne begins. Starring Emilia Clarke, Kit Harington and Peter Dinklage

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: The final episode of the fantasy drama based on George RR Martins A Song of Ice and Fire series of novels. The Great War to gain control over the mythical land of Westeros continues. Starring Emilia Clarke, Kit Harington and Peter Dinklage

programme.title: The White Lotus
programme.desc: Belinda tries to redirect Tanyas focus to her business proposal, Paula grows increasingly disillusioned with the Mossbachers, and Rachel begins to question her future

programme.title: The White Lotus
programme.desc: Rachel shares some harsh truths with Shane and confides in Belinda. Armond goes on an all-out bender and exacts the ultimate revenge on his nemesis

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: As Rhaenyra struggles to come to terms with her sons murder, Alicent grows concerned by Aegons Small Council. Return of the fantasy drama set 200 years before the events of Game of Thrones and telling the story of House Targaryen. Starring Matt Smith and Emma DArcy

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: Rhaenyra questions Daemons loyalty as Otto schemes to turn the public against her, while Ser Criston Cole concocts a misguided plan for revenge

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.desc: As ancient grudges resurface, Rhaenys suggests that Alicent may see reason while the men around her seek bloodshed. Ser Criston proposes a bold scheme, while Daemon arrives at Harrenhal to raise an army for the Blacks

programme.title: House of the Dragon
programme.sub-title: The Red Dragon and the Gold
programme.desc: In Rhaenyras absence and with no word from Daemon at Harrenhal, Rhaenys tries to keep the peace on the Black Council as Cole mounts a campaign into the Crownlands. In Kings Landing, Aemond continues to undermine Aegons fragile hold on authority. Fantasy drama, starring Matt Smith and Emma DArcy

programme.title: Richard E Grants Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: In this edition the actor explores the luxury hotels of Hong Kong. Home to more skyscrapers than any other city, he first checks in to the Mandarin in the financial district, before experiencing the Four Seasons, the only hotel with two Michelin three-star resturants, as well as the timeless Peninsula in Kowloon, which even provides a Rolls-Royce airport taxi service. He ends his stay at the Ritz-Carlton, home to the worlds highest swimming pool

programme.title: Richard E Grants Hotel Secrets
programme.desc: The actor explores Berlin, visiting the arty Propeller Island City Lodge - a hotel where guests can fulfil their fantasies by sleeping in lion cages, coffins or rotating beds. At the Schlosshotel in affluent Grunewald, cabaret dancer Romy Haag talks to Richard about her affair with David Bowie, who stayed at the hotel while living in the German capital in the 1970s

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming discovers more little-known facts on a journey between Smithfields and Spitalfields, two of Londons most famous markets.
