programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The discovery of underwear on a canal bank sparks a risky missing persons search and a police incident ends in a fleeing car passenger jumping over a wall and breaking his leg

programme.title: One Born Every Minute
programme.desc: The documentary follows 32-year-old Naomi, who arrives to give birth wearing false eyelashes and full make-up, and single mother-to-be Bianca, who was shocked to discover she was pregnant a few days after her 22nd birthday. Meanwhile, Shannon and her partner Dale were both 17 when they announced they were expecting a baby and have had to deal with people who claim they are too young for the responsibility

programme.title: One Born Every Minute
programme.desc: Dawn, who endured a traumatic birth in the last series, returns to have another baby, stirring up memories of her difficult experience in the same operating theatre. Meanwhile, 29-year-old Kirri manages without pain relief, laughing her way through her contractions, and Shannon, 34, fears husband Chris may miss the birth of their second child as he must look after their 20-month-old daughter

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: The broadcaster travels to Bedford Hills Correctional Facility in New York State to meet Sheila Davalloo, who has been convicted for murdering Anna Lisa Raymundo. However, in a bizarre twist to the story, Sheila has not yet started her 50-year sentence. First she must finish the 25 years she was given for the attempted murder of her husband. Sheila has remained silent about the killing for 14 years, so Piers tries to get her to finally open up, while also looking into why she wanted both of her victims out of the way

programme.title: Bad Girls Behind Bars
programme.desc: Transgender inmate Franklin falls in love with a fellow prisoner, but they must keep their relationship a secret or face being placed in solitary confinement. A fight breaks out over a carton of milk, and a pregnant prisoner trying to recover from heroin addiction faces losing a place on a treatment programme - which could result in her baby being born in prison and taken from her

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Shooting Joe Exotic
programme.desc: Louis revisits his 2011 documentary Americas Most Dangerous Pets, returning to Oklahoma to explore what has happened since. Joe Exotic, aka Joseph Allen Maldonado-Passage, now resides in a federal prison after being found guilty of a murder-for-hire plot and multiple animal cruelty charges, and having been the subject of hit Netflix documentary Tiger King

Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole gives Simon OBrien a motorcycling masterclass in Wiltshire as the duo seek more items to fix up and sell on to make profits for their owners

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Ever since suffering a brain infection four years ago, Sunderland woman June Findlay has been unable to walk or talk and is totally dependent on her devoted husband John. Since then she has been sleeping on a hospital bed in the kitchen, with no washing or toilet facilities. Joan and John do their best, but Nick Knowles believes they deserve better, so he and his small band of builders - bolstered by an army of local tradespeople - set out to extend the Findlays home, with designer Oliver Heath on hand to protect his vision. Can they give June back her dignity?

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Two years ago, Michelle and Rod Malone set about adapting their home after daughter Maddie contracted a rare brain disease that left her struggling to walk and talk. However, unforeseen problems with the project left them living in just a couple of rooms in an unsafe house. Enter Nick Knowles and the DIY SOS builders, bolstered by an army of local tradespeople, to complete what the Malones started and give it a few finishing touches, courtesy of flamboyant designer Laurence Llewelyn-Bowen

programme.title: My Dream Home
programme.desc: After supporting an elderly relative through a tough time, Carolyn, Brad and their family are searching for a home of their own

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team come to the aid of Manchester couple Rebecca and Steve, whose life was changed for ever when they found out they were expecting triplets. Two years on, Rebecca is becoming depressed by the state of their small, damp, dilapidated house, which is unsafe for the children. The builders have just nine days to bring the property up to date and give this tired mum and dad the space they need for their active offspring. Luckily, local tradespeople and other members of the community turn out in force to help

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Ever since suffering a brain infection four years ago, Sunderland woman June Findlay has been unable to walk or talk and is totally dependent on her devoted husband John. Since then she has been sleeping on a hospital bed in the kitchen, with no washing or toilet facilities. Joan and John do their best, but Nick Knowles believes they deserve better, so he and his small band of builders - bolstered by an army of local tradespeople - set out to extend the Findlays home, with designer Oliver Heath on hand to protect his vision. Can they give June back her dignity?

programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: A major fire breaks out in a haulage depot in Brighouse and a serious accident at an illegal off-road biking site leads to a rider needing urgent hospital treatment

programme.title: Psychopath with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: In 2007, when Paris Bennett was 13 years old, stabbed his four-year-old sister to death, a crime for which he has been behind bars ever since. Now 25 and in his first interview on British television, he tells Piers Morgan how this was no impulsive fit of rage but rather a cold, deliberate act planned as an act of revenge against his mother Charity - who is watching the interview unfold in a nearby room. She has stood by him even though she fears that when he gets out, he could still harbour violent intentions towards her - or even worse, towards his younger brother

programme.title: Stacey Dooley Sleeps Over
programme.desc: Stacey joins former glamour model Carla Bellucci for her 40th birthday weekend. Carla has built her career around creating noisy and controversial headlines about herself and her family, and Stacey discovers why she has chosen a lifestyle which has earned her the title of Britains most hated woman

programme.title: Bad Girls Behind Bars
programme.desc: Heroin addict Missy is regularly visited by a sugar daddy, who gives her the cash she needs to fund her habit. But as she awaits trial for kidnapping, she has to decide whether she is willing to give up a life of drugs and money in order to get her children back. Meanwhile, the romance between transgender prisoner Franklin and fellow inmate Leslie continues to blossom. Only able to meet in educational classes, will their relationship be discovered by the officers?

programme.title: Bad Girls Behind Bars
programme.desc: Educated Julia does not match the typical image of a hardened criminal but has been charged with the murder of her ex-boyfriend. Now she must learn to navigate jail for the first time - all while proclaiming her innocence. Rachel feels the full weight of prison rule after failing a drugs test, while Missy, who is facing trial for kidnapping and home invasion, discovers she could face up to 20 years. How will the mum-of-three cope with such a long term?

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Under the Knife
programme.desc: The roving broadcaster travels to California, the birthplace of plastic surgery, to investigate the obsession with beauty and self-image. In his quest he meets Eve, a domineering stylist and her client Laura, and DJ Kris, who is resorting to liposuction to achieve the six-pack he so desires, leading Louis to consider having a nip and tuck himself

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: The West Midlands Ambulance Service treat a man who stabbed a police dog, a woman with a suspected stroke and a man on his fourth bout of pneumonia

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Gemma and Gorka: Life Behind the Lens
programme.desc: A half-term holiday to Madrid gives the family a break, a chance to soak up some Spanish culture, and a long-awaited first meeting between Thiago and his grandparents

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: Piers travels to Gadsden Correctional Facility near Tallahassee in Florida, where he hears the story of Ashley Humphrey. As a 20-year-old in 2003, she pumped eight bullets into barmaid Sandee Rozzo - a crime for which she is now serving 25 years. Piers tries to understand how she came to commit the crime, wondering whether she was persuaded by her domineering and abusive husband - who was being accused of rape by the victim - or if she is simply a cold-blooded killer who came up with the plan herself

programme.title: Alex Jones: Making Babies
programme.desc: Theres heartbreak and jubilation as Alex meets two couples desperately trying to defy the odds and become parents

programme.title: Alex Jones: Making Babies
programme.desc: In this emotional final episode, Alex comes to the end of her training at Kings and three couples await their results. Will they finally become parents?

programme.desc: With Gorka on dad duty, Gemma makes a TV appearance. Later, Gorka heads to Madrid and we get a sneak peek at the Spanish Strictly Come Dancing - Bailando con las estrellas

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien travel Britain to find items to fix up and flog on to make a handsome profit for their owners. Simon recycles some historic oak, while Henry comes unstuck when he tries to get to grips with an old Ford Escort

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles is in Spalding, Lincolnshire, to meet Dakota Read and her family. The eight-year-old was born with quadriplegic cerebral palsy and her home needs to be made more wheelchair-friendly. Enter the DIY SOS builders, who set to work on designer Gabrielle Blackmans vision, although with just nine days to complete the task, theyll need help. Luckily, the local tradespeople are also out in force, giving their time and their skills for free

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Since his wife died shortly after giving birth to their sixth child, Paul Makings has been valiantly holding the family together as they grieve. But hes facing even more problems - living in a two-bedroom house he has started to extend, and forced to sleep on the sofa. Nick Knowles and the team are joined by more than 100 locals in Misterton, Nottinghamshire, as they take on their biggest home build ever. However, they have to battle against a blizzard to complete the job in nine days

programme.title: My Dream Home
programme.desc: Drew Scott and Jonathan Scott come to the aid of Joe and Jessie, who are searching for a large farmhouse-style home

programme.title: My Dream Home
programme.desc: Drew and Jonathan Scott host the property renovation series. Nicoles music career is taking flight, so the Scott twins try to help her find a great home

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles and the team travel to Hessle, near Hull in East Yorkshire, to transform the home of Darren Hudson, who was left paralysed from the waist down due to a spinal cord injury. After three months of hospital rehabilitation, Darren returned home to his family, but tragedy struck again eight days later when his wife Sarah died suddenly. Since that moment, Darren and his three sons have been living in emotional trauma, with the wheelchair-using dad now the main carer for the family

programme.title: DIY SOS
programme.desc: Nick Knowles is in Spalding, Lincolnshire, to meet Dakota Read and her family. The eight-year-old was born with quadriplegic cerebral palsy and her home needs to be made more wheelchair-friendly. programme.title: 999 Rescue Squad
programme.desc: The discovery of underwear on a canal bank sparks a risky missing persons search and a police incident ends in a fleeing car passenger jumping over a wall and breaking his leg

programme.title: One Born Every Minute
programme.desc: The documentary follows 32-year-old Naomi, who arrives to give birth wearing false eyelashes and full make-up, and single mother-to-be Bianca, who was shocked to discover she was pregnant a few days after her 22nd birthday. Meanwhile, Shannon and her partner Dale were both 17 when they announced they were expecting a baby and have had to deal with people who claim they are too young for the responsibility

programme.title: One Born Every Minute
programme.desc: Dawn, who endured a traumatic birth in the last series, returns to have another baby, stirring up memories of her difficult experience in the same operating theatre. Meanwhile, 29-year-old Kirri manages without pain relief, laughing her way through her contractions, and Shannon, 34, fears husband Chris may miss the birth of their second child as he must look after their 20-month-old daughter

programme.title: Killer Women with Piers Morgan
programme.desc: The broadcaster travels to Bedford Hills Correctional Facility in New York State to meet Sheila Davalloo, who has been convicted for murdering Anna Lisa Raymundo. However, in a bizarre twist to the story, Sheila has not yet started her 50-year sentence. First she must finish the 25 years she was given for the attempted murder of her husband. Sheila has remained silent about the killing for 14 years, so Piers tries to get her to finally open up, while also looking into why she wanted both of her victims out of the way

programme.title: Bad Girls Behind Bars
programme.desc: Transgender inmate Franklin falls in love with a fellow prisoner, but they must keep their relationship a secret or face being placed in solitary confinement. A fight breaks out over a carton of milk, and a pregnant prisoner trying to recover from heroin addiction faces losing a place on a treatment programme - which could result in her baby being born in prison and taken from her

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Shooting Joe Exotic
programme.desc: Louis revisits his 2011 documentary Americas Most Dangerous Pets, returning to Oklahoma to explore what has happened since. Joe Exotic, aka Joseph Allen Maldonado-Passage, now resides in a federal prison after being found guilty of a murder-for-hire plot and multiple animal cruelty charges, and having been the subject of hit Netflix documentary Tiger King

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: A widow reluctantly parts with her husbands treasured collection of Rolls-Royces and Bentleys, and an impulse buyer finds one of the cars a new home

programme.title: Retro Electro Workshop
programme.desc: Shamil buys an 80s Atari which is a more complex fix than it first appears. Rob needs to unravel a previous owners modifications before he can get it working again

programme.title: Retro Electro Workshop
programme.desc: Shamils inspired to buy an original 1980s Sinclair C5 for Rob to repair

programme.title: Masters of Reinvention
programme.desc: Dr Shini and her team are tasked with reinventing a camping trailer from 1950 and a two-seater toboggan from 1960

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: A chance to buy goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels to the steel city of Sheffield where he discovers an early 20th century steam engine which produced armour plates for a Royal Navy warship. Then, Michael heads south into Nottinghamshire and to the former pit village of Eastwood, before arriving in Nantwich, where he takes a dip in the briny

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels between Liverpool and Wales. Along the way he discovers a childhood favourite - a miniature tank engine with three carriages in dark red - made by Frank Hornby, and learns the story of a Welsh countess who lived in Gwrych Castle. In Snowdonia, he also rides the waves with a Welsh surfing champion

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In picturesque Snowdonia, Michael Portillo braves the fastest zip line in the world - stretching 1,500 feet across a vast slate quarry as he begins the last leg of his journey to Caernarfon. En route, he also visits the home of British mountaineering, Pen y Gwryd, to hear how an Edwardian journalist and poet created a climbing community, which grew to include men who would conquer Everest in the 1950s

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his early 20th century Bradshaws Guide, Michael Portillo sets off on the first leg of a trip through Britains industrial heartlands in the footsteps of George V, beginning by travelling from Warrington to Preston

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek Mathewson gives the once-over to a Royale Formula Ford race car, and Paul Mathewson is on a marathon trip to Teesside to pick up a 1953 MGYB

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek Mathewson travels to the Lancashire coast to pick up a rare 1972 Citroen SM, and Dave Mathewson admires a Punto thats two decades old but has just 42 miles on the clock

programme.title: Adolf Hitlers War
programme.desc: Betrayed, Stalin fights the German army in the Soviet states. The German occupation policies lead to the first extermination policies of undesirables

programme.title: Secrets and Spies: A Nuclear Game
programme.desc: Documenting the tensions between East and West, which led to a spying game and an arms race that brought the world to the brink of nuclear war. Featuring archive interviews with KGB spy Oleg Gordievsky, who turned double-agent and began working for the British. Plus, a look at the unpublished letters of Michael Bettaney and interviews with former aides to Margaret Thatcher and Ronald Reagan

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from Portchester Castle in Hampshire, where treasures include an extraordinary set of RAF medals, awarded for extreme bravery to an airman who served in Dunkirk and the Battle of Britain. The team also admires some rare punk clothing by fashion designer Vivienne Westwood and a roll of ornate Arts and Crafts wallpaper destined for the bonfire. Amin Jaffer appraises two valuable paintings by prominent 20th-century Indian artists, Jamini Roy and KH Ara, and explains how each man expressed ideas of Indian identity and nationhood in their works

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the Ulster Folk Museum near Belfast. Treasures brought in to be valued include a set of stained-glass windows decorated with mermaids, piece of costume jewellery found in an antique shop in Australia, an ornately decorated mandolin found concealed in an old cupboard, a device used to make penicillin, a silver milk jug from Kashmir and a huge bell from a Japanese temple

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: A sun-bleached red Vauxhall Astra GTE gives the team a headache. They also buy a classic French moped and catch up on the restoration of a Mini Cooper S that was found in a barn

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Restoring Classics
programme.desc: The team gamble on restoring a Vanden Plas saloon from the 1970s and a childs tricycle from the 1960s. Plus, catch up on the restoration of a rare Sunbeam Lotus

programme.title: Adolf Hitlers War
programme.desc: Betrayed, Stalin fights the German army in the Soviet states. The German occupation policies lead to the first extermination policies of undesirables

programme.title: Secrets and Spies: A Nuclear Game

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow

The team also admires some rare punk clothing by fashion designer Vivienne Westwood and a roll of ornate Arts and Crafts wallpaper destined for the bonfire. Amin Jaffer appraises two valuable paintings by prominent 20th-century Indian artists, Jamini Roy and KH Ara, and explains how each man expressed ideas of Indian identity and nationhood in their works

programme.title: Wilderness with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon voyages across the Coral Triangle, a huge wilderness of tropical seas and jungle clad islands in the south-western Pacific, surrounded by the Philippines, Malaysia and Indonesia. programme.title: Wilderness with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: Simon travels across the Kalahari desert in southern Africa, one of the continents last great unspoilt wildernesses. programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels to the steel city of Sheffield where he discovers an early 20th century steam engine which produced armour plates for a Royal Navy warship. Then, Michael heads south into Nottinghamshire and to the former pit village of Eastwood, before arriving in Nantwich, where he takes a dip in the briny

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels between Liverpool and Wales. Along the way he discovers a childhood favourite - a miniature tank engine with three carriages in dark red - made by Frank Hornby, and learns the story of a Welsh countess who lived in Gwrych Castle. In Snowdonia, he also rides the waves with a Welsh surfing champion

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: In picturesque Snowdonia, Michael Portillo braves the fastest zip line in the world - stretching 1,500 feet across a vast slate quarry as he begins the last leg of his journey to Caernarfon. En route, he also visits the home of British mountaineering, Pen y Gwryd, to hear how an Edwardian journalist and poet created a climbing community, which grew to include men who would conquer Everest in the 1950s

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with his early 20th century Bradshaws Guide, Michael Portillo sets off on the first leg of a trip through Britains industrial heartlands in the footsteps of George V, beginning by travelling from Warrington to Preston

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek Mathewson gives the once-over to a Royale Formula Ford race car, and Paul Mathewson is on a marathon trip to Teesside to pick up a 1953 MGYB

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek Mathewson travels to the Lancashire coast to pick up a rare 1972 Citroen SM, and Dave Mathewson admires a Punto thats two decades old but has just 42 miles on the clock

programme.title: Adolf Hitlers War
programme.desc: Betrayed, Stalin fights the German army in the Soviet states. The German occupation policies lead to the first extermination policies of undesirables

programme.title: Secrets and Spies: A Nuclear Game
programme.desc: Documenting the tensions between East and West, which led to a spying game and an arms race that brought the world to the brink of nuclear war. Featuring archive interviews with KGB spy Oleg Gordievsky, who turned double-agent and began working for the British. Plus, a look at the unpublished letters of Michael Bettaney and interviews with former aides to Margaret Thatcher and Ronald Reagan

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents from Portchester Castle in Hampshire, where treasures include an extraordinary set of RAF medals, awarded for extreme bravery to an airman who served in Dunkirk and the Battle of Britain. The team also admires some rare punk clothing by fashion designer Vivienne Westwood and a roll of ornate Arts and Crafts wallpaper destined for the bonfire. Amin Jaffer appraises two valuable paintings by prominent 20th-century Indian artists, Jamini Roy and KH Ara, and explains how each man expressed ideas of Indian identity and nationhood in their works

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the show from the Ulster Folk Museum near Belfast. Treasures brought in to be valued include a set of stained-glass windows decorated with mermaids, piece of costume jewellery found in an antique shop in Australia, an ornately decorated mandolin found concealed in an old cupboard, a device used to make penicillin, a silver milk jug from Kashmir and a huge bell from a Japanese temple

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Belfast to Portrush, he investigates the work of author and Narnia creator CS Lewis and learns how religion influenced Lewiss work. Michael also visits the Royal Portrush Golf Club to find out how Irelands female population dominated the fairways at the time of his guide

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo continues his journey from Northern Ireland across the water to Scotland. Leaving from the seaport of Larne, he reaches Stranraer and the Mull of Galloway, where in a lighthouse built by railway engineer Robert Stevenson he discovers a magnificent machine, installed at the turn of the 20th-century

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Biologist and extreme angler Jeremy Wade travels to Germany to investigate tales of a beast that has been on the prowl in European waters for the past 700 years. His investigations take him to Spain, where he discovers first-hand that as a consequence of a science experiment gone wrong, the Wels catfish is growing to man-eating proportions and is capable of everything depicted in medieval records

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: A wedding reception held on Elsinby Pier ends in tragedy when a fishing trawler sails badly off course. Meanwhile, Adam faces up to a tough decision and the staff at the hospital prepare for a new arrival as Jill goes into labour. Robert Cavanah and Amy Robbins star

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Part one of an interview with the pundit, activist, barrister and former footballer, who opens up about his life, trauma and controversies

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: The show returns to Cardiff, where beautician Leah is waiting to be swept off her feet by the style choices of either law graduate Lloyd, judo champion Aron or student Daffyd. With only their outfits to help her decide, which lad will Leah choose to join her for a romantic date?

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In London, coffee shop supervisor Holly, singer Millie and care assistant Megan seek out stylish outfits for actor John, in the hope he may select one of them for a date

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Famous faces cook up a storm week by week in a bid to outdo their rivals. Each of the celebrity contestants are paired with a professional chef who will mentor and teach them to prepare restaurant quality dishes. Tonight, the remaining celebrities take on the food of India as they battle to stay in the competition. Presented by Emma Willis and Tom Allen

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Part two of two. The former Republic of Ireland manager opens up about life as a manager, taking the Ireland job twice, the 2002 World Cup, and the infamous relationship with Roy Keane

programme.title: The Six OClock Show
programme.desc: Chat show featuring celebrity interviews, food from leading chefs and guidance on a wide range of topics, from consumer affairs to the latest trends, with viewers invited to contribute with pictures, comments, stories and gossip

programme.title: Gift Wrapped
programme.desc: Quiz show in which three engaged couples get the chance to win their wedding gift list and a dream holiday. The betrothed pairs work together across a series of rounds to see off their opponents. Hosted by Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: A profile of the former Leinster and Italy rugby player, who lost sight in his eye following a horrific injury

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: An interview with the former Crystal Palace and Republic of Ireland defender, who discusses the highs and lows of his career

programme.title: Animal Emergency
programme.desc: A French bulldog arrives at the vets for specialist spinal surgery to help him walk properly. Staff in the large animal department have to decide if a wild seals eye is able to be saved, and nurse Clodagh and vet Myles have to look long and hard to find a magnet in a parrots gizzard

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: In the heart of Central America lurks a marine creature believed to reach 8ft in length and weigh up to 300lbs. Extreme angler Jeremy Wade is taken to breaking point as he re-evaluates everything he knows about fishing in an attempt to catch this monster - which is known to leap into boats and cause fatal injuries

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Emma Willis and Tom Allen host as Katherine Ryan, Christopher Biggins, Linford Christie and Ellie Simmonds produce gourmet dishes using only tinned, frozen and pantry ingredients to secure their place in the next round

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Kieron, Angie, Lynn and Tom pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: A woman is unaware that the home she inherited from her father has a $400k outstanding loan on it and is in danger of being foreclosed.

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: The fossil record shows that the arapaima fish has roamed the waters of the Amazon River since the Jurassic age, and uses its bony tongue to crush prey against the roof of its mouth before swallowing. Biologist and extreme angler Jeremy Wade encountered the species once before and came out second best, so this time he is taking no chances as he investigates every aspect of this huge creature

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: After finding out about the hospitals staffing problems, Jill offers to cut short her maternity leave and return to work, while Matron notices that Catherine is doing her best to avoid Adam. Out in Africa, Jeff faces the horrors of war and makes a promise to a dying woman - but faces difficulties in keeping his word

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: The concluding part of an interview with the Derry legend, who discusses his colourful football career and the place GAA has in society

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: In Cardiff, drama student Josiah, factory worker Daniel and support worker Tomas hope to win a date with make-up artist Chanel. All they have to do is find her the perfect outfit - limited only by time, budget and their own sense of style

programme.title: Dress to Impress
programme.desc: Flight attendant Chloe, law graduate Ivana and trampolining champion Suzie all hope to win a date with businessman Myles. To impress him, they must dress him, as he has no other information to go on than the outfits they choose

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Famous faces cook up a storm week by week in a bid to outdo their rivals. Each of the celebrity contestants are paired with a professional chef who will mentor and teach them to prepare restaurant quality dishes. Tonight, the remaining celebrities take on French cuisine as they battle to stay in the competition. Presented by Emma Willis and Tom Allen

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Tommy Martin interviews the former Republic of Ireland footballer, who reflects on a career that began in the League of Ireland, and saw him move to the top of the English game

programme.title: The Six OClock Show
programme.desc: Chat show featuring celebrity interviews, food from leading chefs and guidance on a wide range of topics, from consumer affairs to the latest trends, with viewers invited to contribute with pictures, comments, stories and gossip

programme.title: Gift Wrapped
programme.desc: Quiz show in which three engaged couples get the chance to win their wedding gift list and a dream holiday. The betrothed pairs work together across a series of rounds to see off their opponents. Hosted by Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Part one of an interview with the pundit, activist, barrister and former footballer, who opens up about his life, trauma and controversies

programme.title: Sport Stories
programme.desc: Part one of two. The former Republic of Ireland manager sits down with Tommy Martin. In the first part, he discusses his playing days, including the glory years of Irelands World Cup campaigns

programme.title: Animal Emergency
programme.desc: Clare native Vincent is attending the animal hospital with Porsche, his once speedy greyhound that he fears is on her last legs. Meanwhile, making the trip from Wicklow is Kealan Doyle and his pet Kunekune pigs Hank and Joey, who are lined up for a badly needed operation. Sapphire, a Shibu Inu dog is brought to the Vet Hospital after a sudden decline in her health, but her medical needs come at a huge financial cost for owner Bernadette

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade travels to Colombia to search for a fish suspected of dragging a honeymooning bride to her death in the Amazon. A key witness turns the investigation on its head after revealing the body was found with a single puncture wound, and Jeremy conducts an experiment to prove his unlikely new suspect is capable of pulling an adult under the water

programme.title: Cooking with the Stars
programme.desc: Emma Willis and Tom Allen host as Abbey Clancy, Pasha Kovalev and Katherine Ryan cook their best date night dishes for their partners Peter Crouch, Rachel Riley and Bobby Kootstra in the first three-way cooking battle of the series. The winner will secure a place in the semi-final while the bottom two will fight for their place in the competition in the cook-off

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh hosts as Tracey from Middlesex, Geoff from Southport, Duran from Sunderland and Nick from Belfast pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Contestants Darren, Kerry, Mustafa and Gugu vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to win a cash prize. Quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Judge Judy
programme.desc: Real-life small-claims cases on issues affecting family life, presided over by outspoken former New York judge Judy Sheindlin

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: A mix of celebrity chat, lifestyle features, advice and competitions

programme.title: The Tonight Show
programme.desc: Comment and analysis on the big stories of the day in Ireland and abroad with a panel of guests covering the key issues

programme.title: Soham: Catching a Killer
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on the murders of Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman in 2002, detailing events from the desperate 14-day police search to the discovery of their bodies

programme.title: Brain Doctors: Inside Neurosurgery
programme.desc: At Beaumont, a groundbreaking Parkinsons surgery offers hope, a patients speech hangs in the balance after a risky surgery, while for one grandfather, standing up is life-changing

programme.title: Trigger Point
programme.desc: After the last attack, and with breakthrough evidence, the counter-terrorism police are close to finding the bomber. Lana faces a reckoning when she uncovers the truth behind the campaign, as the confession she fought for so desperately finally comes

programme.title: Cracker
programme.sub-title: The Mad Woman in the Attic - Part One
programme.desc: Part one of two. First-ever episode of the acclaimed crime drama, starring Robbie Coltrane. Psychologist Eddie Fitz Fitzgerald tries to establish if a man suspected of murdering a woman on a train is genuinely suffering from amnesia, but his shambolic behaviour, which has already prompted wife Judith to leave him, fails to endear him to the detectives investigating the crime. Also starring Christopher Eccleston, Geraldine Somerville, Barbara Flynn and Adrian Dunbar

programme.title: Ireland AM
programme.desc: Magazine programme blending lifestyle interviews and news content with human interest stories, consumer affairs, health, politics, gardening, education, finance, food and fashion

programme.title: Spies of Terror
programme.desc: Thanks to Ulysses cyber-infiltration, Lucie and her team are finally able to identify terrorists planning to carry out and attack in France. Malika decides to return to Syria to mobilise Yassine. Spy thriller, starring Rachida Brakni, Fleur Geffrier, Vincent Elbaz and Pierre Perrier. In French

programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder examines real-life cases, looking at the events leading up to crimes, going through the police investigations and summing up the trials

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Lord Ashfordlys tenants are angered when his new estate manager Jimbo Brady employs the hard-line methods learned in South Africa and raises their rents. Brian Rogers faces eviction, but suspicion is more widespread when Jimbos vehicle is sabotaged. Meanwhile, Peggy believes she has found the explanation for Davids recent behaviour

programme.title: Good Witch
programme.desc: Cassie and Sams much overdue date doesnt go as planned, and they end up stranded on an island; Joy dreams about her parents. Catherine Bnell stars

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: How a team of intrepid engineers keep the London Array, one of the largest offshore wind farms on the planet, maintained and producing electricity whatever the weather

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: Nine celebrities embark on an experimental adventure to Udaipur in India, to test whether they can set up a more rewarding retirement than in the UK. Selina Scott, Susan George, Stephanie Beacham, Syd Little, Peter Dean, Bob Champion, Stanley Johnson and the Krankies are the volunteers. However, while veteran of India Stanley throws himself into the experiment with aplomb, Selina is unsure about the group living arrangements and first-time curry eater Bob struggles with the diet

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: A report from Southeast Asia, of something in the water thats sliced cleanly through a young mans testicles, is enough to lure fishing detective Jeremy Wade to the shores of the Mekong - a river long renowned for its monstrous fish. But what kind of creature could have inflicted such a distinctive, surgical wound? In his quest to expose the unseen mutilator, Jeremy uncovers multiple victims whove been mauled, half-blinded, and even fatally wounded by the same mysterious assailant - a baby-faced killer that appears to be proliferating

programme.title: Dont Look Back in Anger
programme.desc: Headline stories from 2003, accompanied by the music of the time

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: An Army deserter arrives in the village following a bust-up with his sadistic sergeant and causes problems for the Rowans when he starts stealing food to survive. The fugitive is only discovered when Nick investigates a series of break-ins around the village. Rural police drama, starring Nick Berry and Niamh Cusack

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: Vet Nigel discovers a worrying growth on much-loved cow Florence, but will she have a second chance? And Gillian must perform surgery on dog Bailey so that he can be rehomed

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi visits the White Hart in St Albans, Hertfordshire, a reputedly haunted hotel run by husband-and-wife team Ross and Jayne McBeath. Capitalising on its spooky reputation, Jayne organises ghost tours complete with skeletons and cackling witches, but the themed restaurant menu seems to be scaring off the guests. To make matters worse, the McBeaths are facing fierce competition from rival establishments and spend most of their time arguing about how to save their business

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Joe blackmails Robert and Aaron, Kim hires Ross, and Kerry is shocked

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Theo ensures that Todd misses his marathon, Will refuses to join his family in condemning Megan, Adam suffers a dramatic loss of business, and Bernie and Dev find Mal waiting upon their return from holiday

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: The hospital has its hands full when a typhoid epidemic breaks out in the community. Things get worse as the diseases source is traced back to the wards and staff struggle to locate it. Dr Weatherill is forced to reprimand Dr Ormerod after catching him sleeping on the job - but also has startling news for him. Drama, starring Robert Daws and Amy Robbins

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jacks house burns down, forcing him to move in with a colleague. At work, he becomes involved in the search for a kidnapper after a young woman disappears. Long-running crime drama, starring David Jason, Bruce Alexander, John Lyons and Marc Warren

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick and Kate must act quickly when a woman takes an overdose, in the belief that her husband has been falsely imprisoned. Meanwhile, Phil learns Jennifer is pregnant and agrees to marry her, but may not survive his stag night at the pub. Rural drama, starring Nick Berry, Niamh Cusack and Mark Jordan

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective investigates when a seemingly beloved resident of a coastal community is fatally driven off the road, and realises she has to dig up the victims past to have any chance of unmasking the killer. Joined by her faithful team, including DC Kenny Lockhart and new recruit DC Jac Williams, Vera attempts to find the missing link that will crack the case, but finds it difficult to get to the truth when everyone they encounter seems to be protecting a secret. Crime drama based on Ann Cleeves series of novels, starring Brenda Blethyn

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Theo tries a charm offensive on George and Christina, Kit panics when Mal names his attacker, Betsy and Dylan babysit Connie, and Hope warns Sam against listening to Daniel

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Cain explodes with suppressed rage, Tracy is guilty, and Dawn questions Kim

programme.title: Essex County
programme.desc: A story of loss and loneliness tracing the interconnected lives of two families in a rural community. Canadian drama, based on the graphic novel by Jeff Lemire

programme.title: Vigil
programme.desc: Amy confronts one of the crew with her discoveries about their deception, but with her mental health suffering, questions are asked about her fitness to investigate. On land, Kirsten refuses to bow to pressure from the authorities and has concerns that they are orchestrating a cover up. As she closes in on the killer, their capture reveals the grave threat that the vessel faces

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Gina and Phil make the final preparations for their big day, but the grooms uneventful stag night stirs up some pre-wedding nerves. Rob and Peggy prepare a surprise for the soon-to-be-wed couple, and the village grows concerned when an elderly resident mysteriously disappears. Tricia Penrose, Mark Jordon, Jonathan Kerrigan and Gwen Taylor star

programme.title: Good Witch
programme.desc: Cassie and Sams double date with hospital administrator Grant and his girlfriend Monica, doesnt go quite as planned. Martha organizes a Grease themed movie night in the park. Stars Catherine Bell and James Denton. The Party The Party Cassie and Sams much overdue date doesnt go as planned and they end up stranded on an island. Joy dreams about her parents. Catherine Bell and James Denton star

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The creation of Hudson Yards on Manhattan Island, New York, a construction project built over the tracks and railway sidings of Penn Station - literally in thin air

programme.title: The Real Marigold Hotel
programme.desc: Selina Scott, Susan George, Stephanie Beacham, Ian and Janette Tough, Syd Little, Peter Dean, Bob Champion and Stanley Johnson embrace life in Udaipur in India. They receive a grand invitation from the Maharanah and are invited to mingle with Udaipurs high society at the City Palace, ending the week with a trip to the northern state of the Punjab to visit one of Indias most spectacular and holiest cities and the magnificent Golden Temple in Amritsar

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Extreme angler Jeremy Wade is on the trail of horrifying killers from the depths of time. Fearsome, prehistoric beasts are brought back to life using cutting-edge technology and recently unearthed fossils in a quest to find the greatest river monster of all time

programme.title: Mountain Vets
programme.desc: A look at the work of mountain vets at the ancient Kingdom of Mourne, Northern Ireland. Cahir is racing to an urgent call-out from a local pedigree sheep breeder, whose prize ewe is having difficulty in labour, and has to perform an emergency C-section. Nuala diagnoses a deep-rooted infection, finding her only choice is to put the calf down or take on a challenging amputation. Meanwhile, Jennie sees a newborn lamb brought to the clinic by its dedicated young owner, farmer Stephen

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Nick and Kate must act quickly when a woman takes an overdose, in the belief that her husband has been falsely imprisoned. Meanwhile, Phil learns Jennifer is pregnant and agrees to marry her, but may not survive his stag night at the pub. Rural drama, starring Nick Berry, Niamh Cusack and Mark Jordan

programme.title: How to Lose Weight Well
programme.sub-title: From the Master Cleanse to the 5:2 Diet
programme.desc: In Britain, around 11 million people - almost one in six of the population - are on some form of diet; many in a bid to slim down for a particular event. In this programme, Dr Xand van Tulleken and dietician Hala El-Shafie help six people go on the most hyped and written about diets on the market, with the volunteers divided into Crashers on short-term regimens, Shape Shifters on six-week programmes, and Life Changers on four-month plans. In the first episode, Katie and Rachael want to slim down before a weekend getaway, and have just 10 days to do so

programme.title: The Hotel Inspector
programme.desc: Alex Polizzi visits the Godolphin Arms, a hotel in the Cornish seaside resort of Newquay that has been losing money, despite regularly taking a large number of bookings from coach tours. She realises that guests are being drastically undercharged for their stays, and devises a tough new strategy to keep the business afloat - but owner Margaret Ashwood, who has run the establishment for nearly 30 years, is reluctant to change her ways

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Cain explodes with suppressed rage, Tracy is guilty, and Dawn questions Kim

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Theo tries a charm offensive on George and Christina, Kit panics when Mal names his attacker, Betsy and Dylan babysit Connie, and Hope warns Sam against listening to Daniel

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Frankie accidentally injures a man attempting to steal his motorbike, but is stricken with guilt when the thief dies after crashing the vehicle. A woman being treated for severe burns disappears home, where Drs Ormerod and Weatherill discover her family secret, while Alun becomes obsessed by an expensive stereo system. With Scott Taylor, Robert Daws and Amy Robbins

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: Jack is taken hostage while interviewing a suspect, but somehow manages to talk his way out of trouble - quite an achievement for a man whose lack of tact is legendary. Having experienced what its like to look down a gun barrel, he barges into a nearby Army camp where investigators are trying to solve a soldiers murder, but finds his help isnt appreciated. With David Jason, Bruce Alexander, Sam Kelly, Nicholas Jones and Saira Todd

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Kates ex-boyfriend turns up in Aidensfield and finds himself under suspicion for a hit-and-run accident. Meanwhile, Bellamy finds himself in charge of organising a local talent contest. Rural police drama, starring Niamh Cusack and Mark Jordon

programme.title: The Body in the Lake
programme.desc: Jimmy calls Dani asking for help as Matt has run away after an argument, while Chloe shows Dani an unsettling text Zac sent her about Sienna. Dani and Jimmy track down a volatile Matt and Dani and Tims marriage reaches a turning point as she admits she cannot stay with him

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Theo stuns Todd by suggesting they get married right away, Bernie experiences divided loyalties to her son and husband, and Will struggles with being the focus of local gossip

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Charity is concerned, and when Cain has a fit of rage, she tries to reassure him

programme.title: The Serpent
programme.desc: The net begins to close on Charles and Marie-Andree in India,and he hatches a daring plan to escape to South America, but he is becoming reckless and soon everything spirals out of control. In Bangkok, Herman, Angela and the Action Committee face up to life after the Sobhraj case

programme.title: Michael Palin in Nigeria
programme.desc: In the last leg of his journey, Michael heads to the city of Benin, where he learns about the 1897 Raid on Benin, viewing a commemorative statue and visiting Igun Street to see bronze statues being made. Onboard a boat on the River Ethiope, he meets Nigerian filmmaker Chuko Esiri. They discuss Nigerias wildlife and the inspiration that Chuko gets from this location

programme.title: Judge Rinders Crime Stories
programme.desc: Barrister Robert Rinder examines more real-life cases, beginning with the 2001 murder of 15-year-old Danielle Jones, who went missing on her way to school

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Rosie is viciously attacked while attending a broken-down vehicle stranded on the moors. When the body of the driver is discovered nearby, Rob and Rachel are determined to solve the mystery. Their investigation leads to a chilling discovery linking the case to a religious cult near Aidensfield

programme.title: Party Animals
programme.desc: Scott takes time off from work in the wake of Jakes death and is happy to receive a surprise visit from Ashika, but a jealous Sophie tells her rival he is only using her to gain Conservative contacts. Elsewhere, George Morgan receives a public dressing-down about exaggerated tales concerning his upbringing - much to Jamess delight. However, the old-school Tory knows who is to blame and already has revenge in mind. Political drama, starring Andrew Buchan and Shelley Conn

programme.title: Party Animals
programme.desc: When Scott returns to work, he is not happy to be relegated to charity work, but things start to look up when he is paired with new trainee Vienna Lurie. He surprises himself by throwing himself into the task, but his new-found enthusiasm lands him in jail. Dannys mother makes a shock announcement, and Ashika contemplates putting herself forward as a candidate in a forthcoming by-election

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants Billie, Seb, Victoria and Joshua vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as Trevor from Northwich, Emily from Glasgow, Mark from St Helens and Viv from Fareham pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds

Can reigning champion Paul Nicholls hold on to his title, can his former protege Dan Skelton achieve his first championship title, or will Willie make history?

programme.desc: Part two of two. Coleens cross-examination begins, as Tomlinson throws doubt upon the legitimacy behind Coleens claims that Rebekah leaked information about her to the press. Coleen rejects the notion that she revelled in celebratory social media memes after her post went viral, while the Wags husbands Wayne and Jamie have a dispute to settle of their own. With Natalia Tena, Chanel Cresswell and Michael Sheen

programme.title: Bargain Fever Britain
programme.desc: Part one of two. While the economy has struggled in recent years, the discount retail sector has boomed, with an increasing number of people flocking to pound stores and clearance sales, buying in bulk and cutting out coupons. This documentary provides an inside look at four major businesses that are thriving in the current climate - American wholesale giant Costco, car supermarket Motorpoint, footwear chain Shoe Zone, and Approved Food, which sells products that are approaching or already past their best-before dates. Narrated by Jill Halfpenny

programme.title: Monday with Gavan Reilly
programme.desc: Gavan Reilly and guests break down the weeks political diary, key Cabinet issues, major stories shaping the national agenda, and the impact of upcoming decisions.

programme.desc: Teresa throws a party that instantly becomes drama-filled when an unsettling video leaks about Luis. Margaret reveals a shocking secret about Jennifer to the ladies

programme.desc: Jackie learns that Jennifer has been digging for dirt on Evan, and Dolores housewarming party ends in tears when she takes Margarets side over Jennifers

programme.title: Accidental Tourist
programme.desc: Catchphrase and Deal or No Deal host Stephen Mulhern is a man who likes what he knows - and that does not include travelling to exotic locations, trying new foods, or being pushed out of his comfort zone in any way. Now he has asked Ant and Dec to help him finally change his ways and they have decided to send their good friend to South Korea to broaden his horizons. On his trip, Stephen encounters exotic cultures and cuisines, incredible people and places, and exhilarating activities he would never have chosen for himself

programme.title: Kate Middleton: Her Life in Pictures
programme.desc: Documentary offering a portrait of Catherine, Princess of Waless remarkable life by delving deep into the most famous photographs of her.
